title: astragalus rumpens
intro: Astragalus rumpens, also known as the prickly milk vetch, is a perennial legume native to North America, characterized by its distinctive prickly stems and pink or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to boost the immune system and reduce stress levels. The health benefits of Astragalus rumpens include reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced antioxidant defenses, making it a popular herbal remedy for various conditions. Astragalus rumpens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
